tossups.question: During a thunderstorm, a character in this novel nicknamed either the Lunatic or the Sage debates the philosophy of purgatory. The 25th chapter of this novel was omitted from the original edition and is commonly referred to as Chapter X [“ex”]. In this novel, a gravedigger explains throwing the disinterred corpse of a character’s father into a lake by claiming that “it is better to drown than to be with the Chinese.” A 1950s law made this novel and its sequel required reading in schools, though (+) Church opposition meant most students only ever read a heavily redacted version. A young boy in this novel is told to “forget not those who have fallen during the night” by the realist Elías. In this novel, María Clara gives up a farewell letter used to convict Crisostomo Ibarra for a failed rebellion. For 10 points, (*) El Filibusterismo is the sequel to what Filipino protest novel by José Rizal?
tossups.answer_sanitized: Noli Me Tangere [accept Touch Me Not]
tossups.question_sanitized: During a thunderstorm, a character in this novel nicknamed either the Lunatic or the Sage debates the philosophy of purgatory. The 25th chapter of this novel was omitted from the original edition and is commonly referred to as Chapter X ["ex"]. In this novel, a gravedigger explains throwing the disinterred corpse of a character's father into a lake by claiming that "it is better to drown than to be with the Chinese." A 1950s law made this novel and its sequel required reading in schools, though (+) Church opposition meant most students only ever read a heavily redacted version. A young boy in this novel is told to "forget not those who have fallen during the night" by the realist Elias. In this novel, Maria Clara gives up a farewell letter used to convict Crisostomo Ibarra for a failed rebellion. For 10 points, (*) El Filibusterismo is the sequel to what Filipino protest novel by Jose Rizal?

bonuses.leadin: This novel’s depiction of Crisóstomo’s failed revolution and framing by colonial authorities using Maria Clara’s letter later inspired a real revolution. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This novel's depiction of Crisostomo's failed revolution and framing by colonial authorities using Maria Clara's letter later inspired a real revolution. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel. The Rizal law mandates that every high school student read this novel as well as its sequel El Filibusterismo.
Noli me Tangere is a national epic from this country, whose author José Rizal was executed during its revolution.
This Filipino author combined magical realism with Filipino history in his short story “May Day Eve” and novel The Woman who Had Two Navels.
parts_sanitized: Name this novel. The Rizal law mandates that every high school student read this novel as well as its sequel El Filibusterismo.
Noli me Tangere is a national epic from this country, whose author Jose Rizal was executed during its revolution.
This Filipino author combined magical realism with Filipino history in his short story "May Day Eve" and novel The Woman who Had Two Navels.

packet.name: E – Durham A, Florida A, Nebraska A

bonuses.leadin: This author was credited with popularizing a folktale in which a cunning turtle feeds a monkey’s corpse to its friends before tricking them all into drowning. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author who wrote El filibusterismo as a sequel to his novel in which Maria Clara chooses to become a nun after the death of the mestizo Crisóstomo Ibarra.

Nick Joaquin, a Filipino author who wrote about Rizal, used a pen name inspired by this character. An essay by Miguel de Unamuno claims that Rizal is an Oriental version of this gentleman of La Mancha created by Cervantes.
Rizal wrote the poem “Mi último adiós,” or “My Last Farewell,” before dying in this manner. One Hundred Years of Solitude opens with Colonel Aureliano Buendía facing death in this manner.
answers: José Rizal [or José Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda] (The folktale is “The Monkey and the Turtle” and the novel is Noli Me Tángere.)
Don Quixote (The Unamuno essay is “Rizal: The Tagalog Hamlet.” Joaquin’s pen name was “Quijano de Manila.”)
answers_sanitized: Jose Rizal [or Jose Protasio Rizal Mercado y Alonso Realonda] (The folktale is "The Monkey and the Turtle" and the novel is Noli Me Tangere.)
Don Quixote (The Unamuno essay is "Rizal: The Tagalog Hamlet." Joaquin's pen name was "Quijano de Manila.")
firing squad [prompt on execution; prompt on getting shot]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This author was credited with popularizing a folktale in which a cunning turtle feeds a monkey's corpse to its friends before tricking them all into drowning. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this author who wrote El filibusterismo as a sequel to his novel in which Maria Clara chooses to become a nun after the death of the mestizo Crisostomo Ibarra.

Nick Joaquin, a Filipino author who wrote about Rizal, used a pen name inspired by this character. An essay by Miguel de Unamuno claims that Rizal is an Oriental version of this gentleman of La Mancha created by Cervantes.
Rizal wrote the poem "Mi ultimo adios," or "My Last Farewell," before dying in this manner. One Hundred Years of Solitude opens with Colonel Aureliano Buendia facing death in this manner.

bonuses.leadin: In Lavinia Fontana’s rendition of this scene, Jesus is unusually shown with a straw hat. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this scene also painted by Correggio in which Christ is often depicted with some sort of gardening tool. Titian’s early paintings include a version of this scene in which Christ is barely covered with a white cloak.
Jesus is usually shown saying “noli me tangere” to this Biblical woman, who is shown “with a smoking flame” in a chiaroscuro painting by Georges de la Tour (“zhorzh duh la toor”).
This man used wood for an emaciated-looking Penitent Magdalene clasping her hands. This 15th-century sculptor from Florence produced a famous bronze David.
answers: Mary Magdalene [accept The Penitent Magdalene; do not accept or prompt on “Mary” or “Virgin Mary” or “Madonna”]
answers_sanitized: Mary Magdalene [accept The Penitent Magdalene; do not accept or prompt on "Mary" or "Virgin Mary" or "Madonna"]
Donatello [or Donato di Niccolo di Betto Bardi]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In Lavinia Fontana's rendition of this scene, Jesus is unusually shown with a straw hat. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this scene also painted by Correggio in which Christ is often depicted with some sort of gardening tool. Titian's early paintings include a version of this scene in which Christ is barely covered with a white cloak.
Jesus is usually shown saying "noli me tangere" to this Biblical woman, who is shown "with a smoking flame" in a chiaroscuro painting by Georges de la Tour ("zhorzh duh la toor").
This man used wood for an emaciated-looking Penitent Magdalene clasping her hands. This 15th-century sculptor from Florence produced a famous bronze David.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Sisa goes insane and dies after her sons Crispin and Basilio are accused of stealing from a convent. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel, which ends with the death of the revolutionary Elias. It focuses on the relationship between Maria Clara and Cristostomo Ibarra, who is jailed after being falsely accused of being the mastermind behind a revolt.
Noli Me Tangere was written by this Filipino nationalist, who also wrote El Filibusterismo before being shot by a firing squad in 1896.
This antagonist of Noli Me Tangere excommunicates Ibarra after goading him to violence by insulting his father. This corrupt, fat friar is also the illegitimate father of Maria Clara.
answers: Padre Damaso Verdolagas [or Father Damaso Verdolagas; or Damaso Verdolagas]
answers_sanitized: Padre Damaso Verdolagas [or Father Damaso Verdolagas; or Damaso Verdolagas]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: In this novel, Sisa goes insane and dies after her sons Crispin and Basilio are accused of stealing from a convent. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this novel, which ends with the death of the revolutionary Elias. It focuses on the relationship between Maria Clara and Cristostomo Ibarra, who is jailed after being falsely accused of being the mastermind behind a revolt.
Noli Me Tangere was written by this Filipino nationalist, who also wrote El Filibusterismo before being shot by a firing squad in 1896.
This antagonist of Noli Me Tangere excommunicates Ibarra after goading him to violence by insulting his father. This corrupt, fat friar is also the illegitimate father of Maria Clara.

bonuses.leadin: At the end of this novel, readers learn that Father Salvi regularly used Captain Tiago’s daughter, a nun, for sex. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which Frey Damaso convinces Tiago to disallow Maria Clara from marrying the protagonist after that protagonist gets excommunicated from the Catholic Church.
This Filipino author and revolutionary wrote Noli Me Tangere and its sequel, El Filibusterismo.

Ambeth, a scholar with this last name, tried to reconstruct the final novel of Jose Rizal. Other authors with this surname include the only female who helped compile The Book of Fantasy, and a sibling of that author who published the literary magazine Sur.
answers: Ocampo [Those writers are Ambeth, Silvia, and Victoria Ocampo.]
answers_sanitized: Noli Me Tangere [or Touch Me Not]
Ocampo [Those writers are Ambeth, Silvia, and Victoria Ocampo.]

bonuses.leadin: The model for the title high-priced whore of this painting was also the model for the Darnstadt Madonna and is in the same setting as the artist of this painting’s Venus and Amor. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting in which the title woman holds out her hand, likely to request more coins to add to her small pile of gold which is titled with an inscription on the edge of the stone table.
Lais of Corinth was painted by this artist, who included a funky looking skull in his double portrait of Jean de Dinteville and Georges de Selve.

Holbein also painted this scene, in which the resurrected Jesus is urged to stay back, with the title phrase, by a terrified Mary Magadalene, who is holding an urn as two angels sit around in the opened tomb.
